call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Financial Solutions. I see you recently filled out a form online regarding our Signature Credit Card. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card at a rate of 15.99%. It has some fantastic rewards, like 2% cashback on all purchases. Does that sound interesting? 
Eleanor: Hmm, maybe. What's the annual fee? 
Agent: It's $99 a year, but honestly, with the cashback, most customers more than make that back. We also have travel insurance and purchase protection. 
Eleanor: Travel insurance… that’s good. I do travel a bit. 
Agent: Exactly! And it's a really low APR compared to other cards with similar benefits. We can even transfer any existing high-interest debt to this card. 
Eleanor: I do have a balance on another card… 
Agent: Perfect! We can consolidate that for you. So, are you ready to get this card activated? It's a quick process. 
Eleanor: Okay, yeah, let's do it.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Financial Solutions. We're offering a pre-approved Personal Loan. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't ask for this. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… it's a marketing list. We're offering a $10,000 Personal Loan at 18.5%. 
Robert: 18.5?! That's highway robbery! I already have a loan. 
Agent: Well, sir, we might be able to offer a lower rate than your current loan. 
Robert: I doubt it. And frankly, I don't like being called out of the blue. 
Agent: I understand your concern, sir. But just to be sure… 
Robert: No, I'm sure. Don't call me again. *click*

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Financial Solutions. I'm calling about our Signature Credit Card. Your friend, Emily, referred you. 
Jessica: Oh, hey! Yeah, Emily mentioned it. 
Agent: Great! We're pre-approving you for an $8,000 Signature Credit Card at 14.25%. It's got some amazing perks. 
Jessica: Like what? I'm kinda busy, so… 
Agent: Sure, no problem. It has 3% cashback on dining and travel, 1% on everything else, and a really low foreign transaction fee. Plus, a $200 sign-up bonus if you spend $1000 in the first three months. 
Jessica: Okay, that sounds pretty good. What's the annual fee? 
Agent: It's $150 a year. But, we can waive that for the first year. And, uh, there's a late payment fee of $35. 
Jessica: $150 is kinda steep… 
Agent: It is, but the rewards really offset that. We also have a purchase protection plan, and you can earn points towards travel rewards. We can go over the terms and conditions in detail if you'd like. 
Jessica: Maybe. Let me think about it. Can you send me some information by email? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll send you a detailed brochure and a link to the online application. What's your email address? 
Jessica: jessica.chen@email.com

Agent: Hello, Michael? This is Lisa from Financial Solutions. I see you inquired about a Personal Loan on our website. 
Michael: Yeah, that's right. I'm looking to consolidate some debt. 
Agent: Excellent! We can definitely help with that. We're pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 16.75%. 
Michael: 16.75… that's a little higher than I was hoping for. 
Agent: I understand. However, with our loan, you'll have one fixed monthly payment, which can simplify your finances. And, uh, it'll help improve your credit score in the long run. 
Michael: Hmm, okay. What are the fees like? 
Agent: There's an origination fee of 2%, which is $300. But there are no prepayment penalties. 
Michael: No prepayment penalties, that's good. 
Agent: Exactly! And we offer flexible repayment terms, from 3 to 5 years. 
Michael: Okay. Can you send me a breakdown of the monthly payments for different terms? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll email you a detailed amortization schedule right away. Would you like me to proceed with a formal application?

Agent: Hello, Ms. Rodriguez? This is Sarah calling from Financial Solutions. 
Patricia: (Yelling) You guys called me yesterday! I told you I'm not interested! 
Agent: I apologize for the inconvenience, ma'am. We're offering a pre-approved $3,000 Personal Loan at 22.99%. 
Patricia: 22.99?! Are you kidding me? I can't afford that! And I already said I don't want it! 
Agent: We understand your situation, ma'am. We might be able to work with you on the terms… 
Patricia: No! Just take my number off your list! I'm filing a complaint! *click*
